key_change: Does not consider whether US deficits adjust via valuation effects on gross asset positions rather than trade flows alon

challenge_points.description: Analysis relies on one unnamed study while listed NBER papers on US-specific external adjustment and exorbitant privilege remain unexamined.
Assertion of no direct US evidence is overstated given the listed NBER studies that quantify reserve-currency adjustment channels.
No link made to the NBER paper "From World Banker to World Venture Capitalist" on US external adjustment mechanisms.
Does not consider whether US deficits adjust via valuation effects on gross asset positions rather than trade flows alone.

first_pass_summary: The research session examined a single study analyzing 70 current account adjustment episodes across 51 economies from 1980-2024. The core finding is that current account adjustments operate through asymmetric mechanisms: deficit countries adjust primarily through domestic demand compression and import reduction, while surplus countries adjust through different channels (the specific mechanism for surplus countries was truncated in the findings).
